question: How does TypedEventEmitter link event name to payload type?

options.text: Through generic parameter K extends keyof TEvents, where TEvents[K] defines the payload type
Through function overloads for each event
options.explanation: Overloads would require creating a separate signature for each event. The generic approach scales automatically when new events are added to the map

options.text: Through runtime event name checking in switch/case
options.explanation: Runtime checking doesn't provide compile-time type safety. The generic parameter allows TypeScript to check correspondence statically

options.text: Through decorators attached to the class
options.explanation: Decorators don't participate in type inference. The link is provided through indexed access types: TEvents[K]

question: Why does the on() method return an unsubscribe function instead of using off()?

options.text: To prevent memory leaks — cleanup function is easy to use in useEffect and destructors
Because off() is impossible to implement with generic types
options.explanation: off() is implementable with the same generic types. Returning an unsubscribe function is a convenience: no need to store a handler reference

options.text: To improve performance through closures
options.explanation: Performance isn't the reason. The main advantage is ergonomics: cleanup function pairs perfectly with React useEffect and the RAII pattern

options.text: It's a mandatory TypeScript requirement for generic classes
options.explanation: TypeScript doesn't require this. It's an architectural decision for subscription management convenience

question: How does Extract<TEvent, { type: K }> work in the getByType method?

options.text: Extracts from the union type TEvent only the variant whose type field matches K
Creates a new type based on TEvent and K
options.explanation: Extract doesn't create new types; it selects existing union members that are assignable to { type: K }

options.text: Converts TEvent into an array of elements of type K
options.explanation: Extract is a conditional type for filtering unions, not for creating arrays

options.text: Removes the type field from TEvent
options.explanation: That describes Omit, not Extract. Extract selects matching variants from a union

question: Which of the following properties are characteristic of Event Sourcing?

options.text: All changes are stored as immutable events
State is reconstructed through replaying the event sequence
Events are overwritten with each update
options.explanation: In Event Sourcing, events are immutable (append-only). Overwriting violates the audit trail principle and time-travel capability

options.text: Time-travel is supported through replay up to a specific point

question: What happens if a middleware in Event Bus doesn't call next()?

options.text: The event will be blocked — subscribers won't receive notifications
A runtime error will occur because the middleware chain was interrupted
options.explanation: Chain interruption is normal behavior. Middleware can intentionally block events, for example, for validation

options.text: The event will be sent to subscribers directly, bypassing remaining middlewares
options.explanation: Without calling next(), remaining middlewares and final dispatch don't execute. This is the core control mechanism in the middleware pattern

options.text: TypeScript will produce a compile error
options.explanation: next() is an optional call. TypeScript cannot track whether a function will be called at runtime
